evals.prompt: Add a --json flag to this CLI tool. When passed, it should output the word count, character count, and line count as a JSON object instead of the plain text format. Run it against sample.txt to make sure it works.
evals.expected_output: Claude adds the flag, builds the project, runs it with --json and sample.txt, and confirms the JSON output is valid and contains the correct counts.
expectations: Program.cs was modified to support a --json flag
The project was built with dotnet build (or equivalent) and succeeded
The compiled tool was executed with --json flag and sample.txt as arguments
The agent verified the JSON output contains correct word, character, and line counts

evals.prompt: Add a DELETE /tasks/:id endpoint to this API. It should return 204 on success and 404 if the task doesn't exist.
evals.expected_output: Claude adds the endpoint, installs deps, starts the server, creates a task via POST, deletes it via DELETE, and verifies both the 204 response and the 404 for a nonexistent task.
expectations: server.js was modified to include a DELETE /tasks/:id route
The server was started and confirmed to be running without errors
The DELETE endpoint was tested by making actual HTTP requests (curl, fetch, etc.)
Both the 204 success case and the 404 not-found case were verified via actual HTTP responses

evals.prompt: Add a --summary flag to this script. When passed, instead of converting the full CSV, it should print a summary: the number of rows, the column names, and the min/max of any numeric columns. Try it on sample.csv.
evals.expected_output: Claude adds the flag, runs the script with --summary and sample.csv, and verifies the output shows correct row count (3), column names, and numeric range for age (25-35).
expectations: convert.py was modified to support a --summary flag
The script was executed with --summary and sample.csv as arguments
The agent verified the summary output shows correct row count (3), column names, and numeric range for age (25-35)
